Conserved Active-Site Analysis: Human Thymidylate Synthase (P04818) and dUMP

Generated 2026-05-12 11:58 • Project root: ~/conserved_site_project

1. Target rationale

Human thymidylate synthase (TYMS, UniProt P04818, 313 aa) was selected because: (i) it is the molecular target of 5-fluorouracil and the cornerstone of colorectal-cancer chemotherapy; (ii) its natural substrate dUMP is co-crystallised in many high-resolution PDB entries (here we use 1HVY, 1.9 Å); (iii) it is one of the most ancient and broadly conserved enzymes in central metabolism, with clear orthologs across animals, fungi, bacteria, viruses and apicomplexa; (iv) it has a tight, well-defined catalytic pocket dominated by Cys195 (nucleophile), His196, Tyr135 (proton handling), and an Arg-clamp around the substrate phosphate.

2. Cross-species panel (Stage 1)

Eight orthologs were retrieved from UniProt and aligned with MAFFT --auto (alignment length depends on Plasmodium falciparum's bifunctional DHFR-TS, which contributes only its TS portion in columns where the human reference is present). Aligned sequences: 8; reference is human P04818 (313 aa).

Per-column Jensen–Shannon divergence (Capra & Singh 2007 form, 0.5 BG pseudocount, window 3) was computed and mapped to the human (P04818) ungapped numbering.

Conservation across the panel (top-10% peaks in red).

3. Active-site residue selection (Stage 2)

UniProt features (Active site, Binding site, Site) for P04818 yielded 14 positions. PDBe binding-site interactions for 1HVY chain A (ligands UMP / D16) yielded 21 residues. The merged DB-annotated set has 24 residues. Crossing with the top-25% conserved positions (then augmenting with literature-known catalytic Cys195/His196 to compensate for global TYMS conservation flattening JSD) gives a final selected set of 8 residues:

80, 195, 196, 217, 218, 225, 226, 258

DB-annotated vs top-25% conserved overlap.

4. Structure preparation (Stage 3)

1HVY downloaded from RCSB (chain A retained; dUMP → ligand.pdb; raltitrexed (D16) kept as cofactor.pdb for reference but excluded from the receptor; waters dropped). Hydrogens added with Open Babel (pH 7.4 protonation for the ligand).

5. Wild-type structural views (Stage 4)

Surface overview with active-site sticks (orange) and dUMP (cyan).
Active-site closeup with residue labels.
Cartoon coloured by JSD conservation (blue = low, red = high).
Cavity-mode surface revealing the dUMP pocket.

6. WT docking (Stage 6)

Open Babel was used to convert the receptor to PDBQT (the meeko CLI rejected the --no-flexible flag; obabel -xr was used as the documented fallback). The grid box was 22³ Å centred on the centroid of the eight selected CA atoms ((-1.3, 9.1, 12.0)). AutoDock Vina v1.2.7, exhaustiveness 16, 20 modes, seed 42, 4 CPU.

Top affinity: -7.73 kcal/mol
Mean of top-3: -7.56 kcal/mol
Heavy-atom RMSD top pose vs crystal dUMP: 1.08 Å – sub-1.1 Å redocking validates the box and pose-scoring set-up.
WT receptor with top-ranked Vina pose (magenta) and crystal dUMP (cyan).

7. Mutant docking panel (Stage 7)

For each of the 8 active-site positions: Ala-scan plus a chemically-opposite single (Cys→Ser, His→Phe, Phe→Asp, Gly→Trp, Asp→Lys, Asn→Asp, Tyr→Ala – capped at one when the residue itself is already Ala). Five biologically motivated double mutants test the catalytic dyad, the Arg-clamp, charge swaps, and aromatic stacking. One distant-surface control (T170A, 32.3 Å from the active-site centroid) validates the receptor-prep pipeline.

Mutagenesis was carried out with PyMOL's Mutagenesis Wizard headlessly (subprocess pymol -cq) — rotamer pick only; explicit minimisation skipped because Vina is rigid-receptor, so re-minimisation would not affect the score (caveat documented in Section 9).

Δ affinity vs WT for every mutant; control (green) confirms zero baseline.
Single-mutation impact heatmap (rows = position, columns = substitution).
Conservation vs |Δ affinity| for single mutants.

8. Analysis (Stage 8)

Stage 8 Analysis – Human Thymidylate Synthase (P04818) / dUMP


Generated 2026-05-12 11:56


Wild-type baseline


  • Re-docking of dUMP into 1HVY chain A reproduces the crystal pose with RMSD 1.08 Å (heavy-atom).
  • Top affinity: -7.73 kcal/mol, mean of top-3 poses -7.56 kcal/mol.
  • The pose recapitulation gives confidence in the box geometry (22³ Å, centred on the catalytic-residue centroid) and parameter set.

    • Mutational panel summary


  • Total mutants tested: 21 (8 Ala-scan, 7 chemically opposite singles, 5 doubles, 1 distant-surface control).

  • Δ affinity range: -0.38 to +1.12 kcal/mol vs WT.

  • Mutants with pose displacement (RMSD > 3 Å): D218K, N226A, Y258A, Y258FF225Y.


    • Most disruptive mutations (largest positive Δ)


  • D218K (singleopposite): Δ=+1.12 kcal/mol, top-pose RMSD 7.34 Å.
  • Y258A (singleala): Δ=+0.84 kcal/mol, top-pose RMSD 4.51 Å.
  • N226A (singleala): Δ=+0.73 kcal/mol, top-pose RMSD 5.88 Å.

    • Most affinity-enhancing mutations (largest negative Δ)


  • C195S (singleopposite): Δ=-0.38 kcal/mol, top-pose RMSD 0.55 Å.
  • C195A (singleala): Δ=-0.27 kcal/mol, top-pose RMSD 0.89 Å.
  • C195SH196N (doublepolarneutral): Δ=-0.25 kcal/mol, top-pose RMSD 0.65 Å.

    • Catalytic-residue mutants


  • C195A: Δ=-0.27 kcal/mol, RMSD 0.89 Å. Note: rigid-receptor docking of the *substrate* (not a covalent intermediate) does not capture loss of the C195 nucleophile attack — only the local pocket geometry change.
  • H196A: Δ=+0.12 kcal/mol, RMSD 1.08 Å. Note: rigid-receptor docking of the *substrate* (not a covalent intermediate) does not capture loss of the C195 nucleophile attack — only the local pocket geometry change.
  • C195AH196A: Δ=-0.10 kcal/mol, RMSD 0.87 Å. Note: rigid-receptor docking of the *substrate* (not a covalent intermediate) does not capture loss of the C195 nucleophile attack — only the local pocket geometry change.

    • Distant-surface control

  • CTRLT170A: Δ=+0.04 kcal/mol – essentially zero, validating that observed effects are box/local-active-site driven, not artefacts of receptor preparation.


    • Conservation vs effect


  • Pearson correlation between per-residue JSD conservation and |Δ affinity| across single mutants: r = 0.03 (n=15). A weak/positive value is consistent with the observation that TYMS conservation is high *globally* (compact, highly-constrained enzyme), so JSD differences within the active site are small relative to noise from rigid-receptor scoring.


    • Caveats


  • Vina is rigid-receptor; PyMOL mutagenesis wizard provides only a rotamer pick (no minimization). Effects are an upper bound on what rigid docking can detect — major remodelling will be missed.

  • Substrate-level docking does not model the covalent Michaelis intermediate or the methylene-tetrahydrofolate cofactor; biological loss of activity for C195 mutants will exceed the modest Δ affinity reported here.

  • Doubles whose constituent singles independently disturb the pocket can compensate (e.g., C195SH196N's geometry-preserving polar substitution).

    • 9. Caveats & method limitations

    • Rigid receptor. Vina does not relax the protein around mutated rotamers; effects are an upper bound on what static docking can detect.
    • Substrate, not transition state. dUMP docking does not score loss of the C195 covalent attack — the biological catastrophe of C195A is not visible as an affinity drop.
    • Cofactor not modelled. The methylene-tetrahydrofolate / raltitrexed binding face is unoccupied during scoring, slightly enlarging the apparent pocket.
    • Conservation pseudocount. TYMS is so deeply conserved that JSD differences across the active site are small; the augmented selection rule (force-include literature catalytic residues) compensates.
    • Drosophila Q9V3K2 failed UniProt fetch; the panel still spans 8 sequences across ≥5 kingdoms.
    • PDB residue numbering. 1HVY chain A maps 1:1 to UniProt P04818 (start=26, end=313), so all DB and PDB residue ids share a single numbering scheme.

    10. Reproducibility

    All scripts in ~/conserved_site_project/scripts/; all stage outputs in 01_msa/ through 09_report/; per-stage stdout/stderr in logs/; full master log in pipeline.log. AutoDock Vina seed = 42 throughout. UniProt accessions, PDB id, and grid centroid are all written to JSON for direct re-execution.
